articles.title: Dashboard Overview
articles.overview: The Dashboard is the main control center of the system. It provides a real-time overview of your business performance, operational activity, and system health.
articles.content:
The dashboard displays key metrics such as total revenue, number of completed orders, pending orders, and overall activity across the platform. These metrics update automatically, allowing managers and administrators to monitor business performance without generating manual reports.

Every order processed through the system can be monitored from the dashboard. You can see which orders are newly created, currently being processed, in delivery, or already completed. This helps staff respond quickly to delays or operational issues.

Financial widgets display balances related to company wallets, shop payments, and customer transactions. This makes it easier to quickly identify revenue flow and monitor financial health.

The dashboard also highlights alerts such as overdue deliveries, system notifications, or unresolved support tickets. These alerts help teams react quickly and maintain smooth operations.

example.scenario: A manager opens the dashboard at the start of the day.

articles.explanation: This means the business generated $5,760 in revenue today based on the current orders.

articles.title: First Steps
articles.overview: Follow these steps when logging into the system for the first time.
articles.content:
When you first access the system, there are several important steps to help you get started efficiently.

Step 1: Log in to Your Account
Use your assigned username and password to access the secure dashboard. Only authorized users with valid credentials can access the system.

Step 2: Explore the Navigation Menu
The sidebar menu provides access to all system modules including Orders, Products, Inventory, Financials, and Settings. Spend some time familiarizing yourself with where each function is located.

Step 3: Configure Notifications
Enable system notifications to stay informed about new orders, support tickets, low inventory alerts, and payment confirmations.

Step 4: Review Documentation
Visit the Help Center and documentation sections to learn about advanced tools such as promotions, referrals, analytics, and reporting.

example.scenario: A new store manager logs in for the first time and checks today's order activity.

articles.title: Manual Product Entry
articles.overview: Products can be added manually when introducing new items to the catalog or inventory.
articles.content:
Products can be added manually using the 'Add New Product' form. This method is useful when introducing new items or managing small inventories.

Choose an existing product from the global catalog or create a new product name if the item does not already exist. The global catalog helps maintain consistency across shops.

Enter the base cost of the product and the final selling price. The base price is typically what the shop pays, while the selling price is what the customer will see.

Specify the available stock quantity and measurement unit such as pieces, kilograms, or liters. You can also set a reorder level so the system alerts you when stock becomes low.

Toggle the 'Active' status to control whether the product is visible in the marketplace or POS system.
articles.explanation: Each time this product sells, the business earns $4 before operational costs.

articles.title: Bulk Import via CSV
articles.overview: For businesses with large product catalogs, the CSV import tool allows you to add hundreds or thousands of products at once.
articles.content:
Prepare a spreadsheet containing product information such as Product Name, Barcode, SKU, and Description. Save the file in CSV format before uploading it to the system.

The system automatically checks barcodes and SKUs to prevent duplicate entries. If a product already exists, the system will skip or update the record depending on the import settings.

Imported products are added to the Global Product Names database. This allows different shops or branches to use the same standardized product information.

During the import process, the system shows a progress indicator and provides a summary of successfully imported items, skipped entries, and any errors.

articles.title: Creating an Order
articles.overview: Orders can be created through the POS system or automatically through the marketplace.
articles.content:
Orders can be created either manually by staff or automatically through integrated checkout systems.

Choose products from the available inventory list. The system will automatically display prices and available stock.

Enter customer details such as name, phone number, and delivery address. If the customer already exists in the system, their information can be selected from the saved database.

Select the appropriate payment method including Cash, Card, or Mobile Money (MoMo). The system records the selected payment type for accounting and reporting purposes.

After reviewing all details, confirm the order. The system generates an invoice which can be printed or shared digitally with the customer.
articles.explanation: The customer pays $16 in total including delivery.

articles.title: Advanced Barcode Scanning
articles.overview: The barcode scanning feature helps speed up product selection during checkout and inventory management.
articles.content:
Using your device camera, the system can scan product barcodes in real time. Once scanned, the product is automatically identified and added to the cart or product list.

The system searches the Global Product Catalog for the scanned code. If the product exists, its information is instantly retrieved.

If the camera cannot access the barcode, users can manually enter the code into the search field. The system will suggest matching products.

articles.title: Wallet Operations
articles.overview: Wallets track all money movements inside the platform.
articles.content:
Payout Processing: Businesses or partners can request payouts from their wallet balance. Administrators can approve or process these withdrawals from the financial dashboard.

Transaction Reconciliation: All wallet transactions can be reviewed and compared with orders, refunds, and deposits to ensure accurate accounting.

Multi-Currency Support: The system can be configured to support different currencies depending on the region where the platform operates.
articles.explanation: The shop receives $40 while the platform earns $10 commission.

articles.title: Refunds Management
articles.overview: The refund management system allows administrators to process financial reversals when orders need to be cancelled or corrected.
articles.content:
Refunds can be initiated directly from the order details page. Once approved, the system reverses the payment according to the original payment method.

If the platform uses internal wallets, refunded amounts are automatically credited back to the correct wallet account.

Every refund transaction is logged with timestamps, responsible staff members, and related order details. This ensures full transparency during financial audits.

articles.title: Tax Declarations
articles.overview: The system automatically calculates and organizes tax data required for monthly reporting.
articles.explanation: At the end of the month, the business must declare $21,600 in VAT collected.

articles.title: Recipe & BOM Management
articles.overview: Define exactly how products are created from raw materials, essential for restaurants and manufacturing.

articles.title: Production Orders
articles.overview: Plan and track the manufacturing process while ensuring inventory accuracy.

articles.title: Supplier & RFQ
articles.overview: Manage vendor relationships and streamline the process of requesting quotes.

articles.title: Purchase Orders
articles.overview: Formalize buying processes from order creation to receiving goods.

articles.title: Billing Dashboard
articles.overview: Monitor all business subscriptions and upcoming renewal deadlines.

articles.title: Creating Support Tickets
articles.overview: Support tickets allow users or staff members to report issues or request assistance.
articles.content:
Navigate to the Support module in the main menu.

Click the 'New Ticket' button and select the category that best describes your issue.

Provide a clear explanation of the issue and include any relevant details such as order numbers or user IDs.

You can upload screenshots, documents, or images that help explain the problem.

Once submitted, the ticket will be assigned to the appropriate support team for resolution.

articles.title: Tracking Issue Resolution
articles.overview: Monitor your support requests from submission to resolution.
articles.content:
Each ticket displays a full timeline showing when it was created, updated, or responded to by the support team.

Tickets move through several statuses such as Open, In Progress, Awaiting Response, and Resolved.

Users can exchange messages with support agents directly within the ticket thread.

Once a ticket is resolved, users may rate the support experience and provide feedback to help improve service quality.
